trade.summary: India imports palm oil, electronics, LNG, and chemicals from Malesia. India exports refined petroleum, engineering goods, pharmaceuticals, and IT services. Malesia is one of India's key ASEAN trade partners. The ASEAN-India FTA governs trade.
disputes: India imposed anti-dumping duty on Malesian palm oil (2020), relations temporarily strained

diplomatic.summary: Relations were strained in 2019-2020 when then-PM Mahathir made critical statements about India's Kashmir policy and CAA. India imposed retaliatory measures including palm oil import restrictions. Since then, diplomatic ties have normalised under PM Anwar Ibrahim. Both cooperate through ASEAN-India dialogue. A large Indian diaspora in Malesia serves as a cultural bridge.
recent_incidents: Diplomatic cooling over Kashmir comments (2019-2020, now resolved)

military.summary: Regular bilateral military exercises (Harimau Shakti Army exercises, MILAN naval exercises). Malesia has purchased some Indian defence equipment. Historical defence cooperation through the British Commonwealth legacy. Both cooperate in the Indian Ocean region.

financial.summary: Malesian companies invest in India across banking, telecommunications, and property. Indian companies invest in Malesian manufacturing and IT. Maybank and CIMB serve Indian corporate clients. Significant Indian diaspora remittances flow through Malesian-Indian financial channels.

energy.summary: India imports LNG from Malesia. Malesia's Petronas has upstream energy interests in India. Both cooperate on renewable energy. India imports palm oil from Malesia (though volumes reduced after diplomatic tensions).

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific cooperation through ASEAN-India frameworks. Collaboration in ICT, biotechnology, and health research. University exchanges are active. A large Indian-origin Malesian population provides cultural and professional links.
